evidence.deterministic: No randomness, no race conditions — sequential initialization

proofs.notes: Startup is sequential: env load → DB client → repositories → server → listen. No parallelism, no race conditions. fail_closed mode rejects startup without credentials.

mechanisms: Submission: idempotency_key on picks table prevents duplicate submissions
Outbox: idempotency_key on distribution_outbox prevents duplicate enqueue
Delivery: confirmDeliveryAtomic wraps markSent + lifecycle + receipt in one Postgres transaction
Settlement: corrects_id FK — corrections append, never mutate originals
Provider offers: upsert on idempotency_key for dedup
Receipts: idempotency_key format {outboxId}:{target}:receipt prevents duplicate receipts

proofs.notes: Idempotency enforced via idempotency_key columns (picks, outbox, offers, receipts) + 5 atomic Postgres RPC methods + unique indexes. Corrections append-only via corrects_id FK.

proofs.control: All critical services have health checks

evidence.worker_watchdog: Watchdog timer (30s default) kills hung deliveries
evidence.stale_reaper: Stale claim reaper releases stuck rows after 5min

proofs.notes: API has /health endpoint. Worker has heartbeat (touchClaim every 5s) + watchdog (30s) + stale claim reaper (5min). ops:health and ops:brief provide system-level health.

proofs.control: Retry logic exists for transient failures

evidence.retry_mechanism: Retryable failures stay in outbox → next poll cycle retries automatically
evidence.dead_letter: After max attempts → dead_letter status (surfaced in ops digest)
evidence.stale_claim_reaper: Releases stuck processing rows back to pending after 5min

proofs.notes: Failures classified as retryable (5xx/429/network) or terminal (4xx). Retryable stays in outbox for next cycle. Circuit breaker (5 failures → 5min cooldown). Dead letter after max attempts.

proofs.control: System shutdown does not corrupt state

evidence.api_graceful: SIGINT: true, SIGTERM: true, graceful shutdown: true
evidence.api_shutdown: process.once(SIGINT/SIGTERM) → shutdown() → server.close() → 5s timeout → forced exit
evidence.atomic_operations: All critical writes use atomic RPCs — crash between steps cannot leave partial state
evidence.outbox_safety: Outbox claims auto-expire via stale claim reaper — crash releases row back to pending
evidence.no_corruption: Postgres transactions ensure either full commit or full rollback — no partial writes

proofs.notes: Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM with 5s timeout. Atomic RPCs prevent partial state. Outbox claims auto-expire on crash. Postgres transactions ensure no corruption.

proofs.control: All critical services expose structured logs
proofs.notes: 6/6 apps use structured logging via @unit-talk/observability. Already proven in UTV2-683.
